subject: Statistics
title: The Poisson Distribution
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Poisson Distribution.

hook.text: How many emails arrive in the next hour? How many cars pass a bridge in ten minutes?

buildup.text: The Poisson models counts of events in a fixed interval — when events happen randomly.

discovery.text: It needs just one number: the average rate. From that, it predicts 0, 1, 2, or more events.

twist.text: It was invented in 1898 to study Prussian cavalry deaths by horse kick. The fit was perfect.

climax.text: Server crashes, customer arrivals, and radioactive decay all follow Poisson patterns.

punchline.text: One rate predicts an infinity of outcomes.

quiz.question: What input does the Poisson distribution require?
options: Just the average rate of events in a fixed interval
The standard deviation of the data
